title: Soft ordering for roadmaps: distinguish preference (after) from dependency (blocked_by)
description:
Roadmaps currently express two distinct couplings with one edge type: temporal/priority preference (do A before B) and technical dependency (B needs A). Both are stored in blocked_by, and every consumer treats them as hard: query.Ready excludes any tick with an open blocker, so an epic that is merely later in preferred order can never surface via tk next while a long-running predecessor is open — the agent loop cannot skip ahead to the first feasible epic. The current workaround (leave the later epic unlinked) loses the roadmap ordering: the epic floats to wave 0 as if it were the front.

This epic adds a second, soft edge type: a new after field ([]string of tick IDs) on the tick, sibling to blocked_by.

Decided semantics:
- blocked_by = feasibility (hard). Unchanged everywhere; never ready until blockers close.
- after = preference (soft). Affects ordering only, never feasibility. A tick whose after-targets are open is still ready/plannable; it sorts behind candidates whose after-targets are all closed. Skipping ahead needs no flag — it is what happens when preference and feasibility disagree.
- Missing or closed after-targets are ignored (same semantics as missing blockers in ready.go).
- ComputeRoadmap layers waves on the UNION of epic-only blocked_by + after edges so the visual chain preserves intended order; the queued status keeps consulting hard edges only. No new status values — a soft-deferred epic renders ready/active, and renderers annotate the edge type.
- The field is generic (valid on tasks too), but only epic-to-epic edges affect roadmap layering, mirroring blocked_by.

Deliverables: after in data model + JSON schema; --after on tk create/update + tk show display; ComputeRoadmap union layering + After on RoadmapEpic; soft-order-aware selection in tk next (ready pool and planning fallback). Rendering surfaces (CLI/TUI/web board) and skill docs are the follow-on surfaces epic.
notes:
2026-06-12 07:32 - RETRO REPORT — Epic 4bt (soft ordering: after vs blocked_by)
Learnings: 1 new orchestration entry (worktree agents can branch from stale base — name prerequisite SHA, cherry-pick never re-implement; bit us live: 2 agents cherry-picked cleanly, 1 re-implemented causing a benign doc-comment conflict). 1 refinement to Schema codegen entry (tick authors must spell out codegen commands in schema-touching ticks — the 8up omission surfaced only at review). File ~97 lines, under cap.
Outside-in verification: after field round-trips VERIFIED (5 subtests); --after create/update/show VERIFIED (4 ExecuteArgs tests + live use below); union wave layering + hard-only queued VERIFIED (5 table cases + live: e7c layers wave 3 behind o16 yet status active); soft-order tk next selection VERIFIED (A/B/C skip-ahead e2e test); merge driver After union VERIFIED (review fix + test).
Dogfood: converted the e7c<-o16 edge from blocked_by to after at close-out, exactly as flagged in e7c description — live roadmap confirms layering-without-queueing.
Final review: 0 blockers; 2 should-fix fixed in-line (merge-driver union, codegen refresh); 5 nits accepted, three routed to o16 planning notes (awaiting-mode soft-order test, --after StringSlice parity, self-reference validation candidate).
Known gap (in o16 scope by design): tk roadmap <epic-id> chain filter follows hard edges only, so soft-linked epics drop out of the scoped view — recon fact recorded on the o16 CLI tick.
closed_reason: Completed: after field + CLI + union roadmap layering + soft-order tk next, 0-blocker review, all 6 ticks closed
